wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Flow-On project overview section focused on introducing this professional Windows voice-to-text tool. Explain its purpose as a local-first, zero-cloud, zero-telemetry solution that provides real-time speech-to-text functionality. Document the core value proposition of offline AI-powered transcription with sub-3 second latency. Describe the target audience (professionals, developers, content creators) and primary use cases. Include the key differentiators from cloud-based alternatives. Provide an architectural overview showing the relationship between audio capture, AI transcription, visual feedback, and text injection components. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'Alt+V hotkey', 'system tray integration', and 'Whisper.cpp backend'. Include practical examples demonstrating common workflows like quick dictation, code transcription, and snippet usage. Document the technology stack overview covering C++20, miniaudio, whisper.cpp, Direct2D, and Windows API integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for Flow-On focused on rapid onboarding and initial setup. Document the complete installation process from source code compilation to first successful transcription. Include detailed prerequisites (Windows 10/11 x64, Visual Studio 2022, CMake 3.20+, PowerShell) and step-by-step build instructions using the automated build script. Explain the two installation paths: building from source with automatic model download or using the standalone installer. Provide clear first-run instructions including settings.json creation, model downloading (~75MB), and initial hotkey testing with Alt+V. Include troubleshooting for common setup issues like missing dependencies, audio device problems, and installer failures. Document the expected performance characteristics on first run and provide guidance for verifying successful installation. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including 'Alt+V hotkey', 'system tray integration', and 'Whisper.cpp backend'. Include practical examples of basic usage scenarios and next steps for advanced configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Machine Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Flow-On finite state machine architecture. Explain the four-state design (IDLE, RECORDING, TRANSCRIBING, INJECTING) and how atomic state transitions coordinate the entire transcription workflow. Document the state transition diagram showing how user input, audio capture completion, and transcription results drive state changes. Detail the atomic operations used for thread-safe state management and the memory ordering guarantees. Explain how the state machine prevents race conditions during hotkey processing, audio capture, and text injection phases. Include the role of the global state variable in coordinating component interactions and how state changes trigger UI updates, system tray icon changes, and overlay animations. Document the error handling mechanisms that ensure the system returns to IDLE state from any intermediate state. Provide examples of state transition timing and latency measurements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Manager
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Audio Manager component. Thoroughly explain the PCM audio capture implementation using miniaudio library, including device initialization at 16kHz mono sampling rate. Document the circular buffer management system for up to 30 seconds of audio storage, buffer draining mechanisms, and audio data transfer between threads. Detail the noise reduction techniques, RMS calculation for audio level monitoring, and dropped sample detection. Explain the callback-based architecture with SampleCallback function, thread safety considerations, and performance optimization strategies. Include practical examples of audio capture setup, buffer management, and integration with the transcription pipeline. Document error handling, device enumeration, and fallback mechanisms for different audio hardware configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Latency Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for latency optimization in Flow-On's real-time speech-to-text system. Explain the current latency characteristics including audio capture latency (~100ms), transcription processing time (12-18s for 30s audio), and total system latency. Document the specific optimization techniques used including the tiny.en model selection for speed, reduced audio context (512 frames vs default 1500), single-segment mode for dictation workflows, and disabled timestamp generation. Detail the lock-free audio processing pipeline using moodycamel::ReaderWriterQueue for zero-blocking operation. Explain the multi-threaded approach with CPU thread allocation and how it balances speed against UI responsiveness. Include practical examples of latency measurement techniques and provide guidelines for identifying latency bottlenecks. Address the trade-offs between latency and accuracy, and provide recommendations for different use cases requiring varying levels of performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hotkey System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Alt+V hotkey system covering the complete state machine implementation. Document the four-phase state transition system (IDLE → RECORDING → TRANSCRIBING → INJECTING) with detailed timing and behavior descriptions. Explain the atomic guards preventing race conditions between hotkey release and voice activity detection. Detail the hotkey registration process, modifier key handling, and VK code configuration. Cover the visual feedback system during each phase including blue waveform bars for recording, spinner arc for transcription, green flash for successful injection, and red flash for errors. Document the auto-hide functionality after 2 seconds of completion. Include troubleshooting guidance for common hotkey conflicts and registration failures. Provide configuration examples for customizing the hotkey combination and modifiers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Manager API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the AudioManager class interface. Document the constructor and initialization methods including init() function with SampleCallback parameter for audio data processing. Detail the audio capture control methods startCapture(), stopCapture(), and drainBuffer() for managing recording sessions. Explain the audio buffer management functionality including the pre-allocated 30-second buffer for 16kHz mono audio. Document the audio metrics accessors getRMS(), getDroppedSamples(), and resetDropCounter() for monitoring audio quality. Cover the internal callback mechanism onAudioData() and thread safety considerations. Include method signatures, parameter descriptions, return values, and usage examples. Address memory management, performance considerations, and integration patterns with the audio processing pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the getting started section focused on helping users quickly set up and begin using Flow-On. Document the complete installation process from source code compilation to running the application. Include detailed prerequisites covering Windows 10/11 x64, Visual Studio 2022, CMake 3.20+, and PowerShell requirements. Provide step-by-step build instructions using both automated PowerShell scripts and manual CMake workflows. Document the build options including Release configuration, CUDA support, and NSIS installer generation. Explain the first-time setup process including automatic model downloading, settings.json creation, and system tray integration. Include basic usage instructions covering the Alt+V hotkey functionality, visual feedback system, and initial configuration steps. Address common installation issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide verification steps to ensure proper installation and operation. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for developers who want to build from source.

wiki_catalogs.name: Features Showcase
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive features showcase content for Flow-On focused on highlighting the complete feature set and user experience. Document each major feature with practical examples and use cases: the Alt+V hotkey system for seamless dictation, real-time GPU-accelerated waveform overlay with Direct2D rendering, Whisper.cpp backend with offline AI transcription, smart text injection with context-aware formatting, minimalist system tray interface, intelligent snippet engine for text substitution, and standalone installer with all dependencies. Explain the technology behind each feature including miniaudio for audio capture, Direct2D for graphics, and Windows API integration. Provide concrete examples of how features work together in real-world scenarios like coding transcription with camelCase transforms, normal text cleanup with punctuation restoration, and snippet expansion for boilerplate text. Include performance metrics and system requirements for each feature. Document the user workflow from activation to text injection and explain the visual feedback system with state indicators. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and provide screenshots or descriptions of the overlay states (recording, processing, done, error).

wiki_catalogs.name: Threading Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Flow-On threading architecture. Explain the multi-threaded design with separate threads for audio capture, AI processing, UI rendering, and text injection. Document the message-only window thread that handles Windows API callbacks and user interactions. Detail the audio callback thread that processes PCM data in real-time and manages the circular audio buffer. Explain the worker thread model used by the transcriber for asynchronous Whisper processing. Document the thread synchronization mechanisms including atomic variables, memory barriers, and message passing between threads. Cover the thread safety considerations for shared resources like the audio buffer, state variables, and configuration settings. Include the timer-based polling mechanism for detecting hotkey releases and how it interacts with the main message loop. Address thread cleanup during application shutdown and memory protection measures to prevent data leakage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transcriber
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Transcriber component. Thoroughly explain the whisper.cpp integration architecture, including model loading, quantized model management, and inference pipeline setup. Document the GPU acceleration configuration with CUDA support, CPU fallback mechanisms, and performance optimization strategies. Detail the asynchronous transcription processing model, threading architecture, and inter-thread communication patterns. Explain the audio preprocessing steps, feature extraction, and text generation workflow. Include practical examples of model initialization, transcription execution, and result handling. Document configuration options for model quality, language settings, and performance tuning. Address memory management, model caching, and resource cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: GPU Acceleration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for GPU acceleration implementation in Flow-On using NVIDIA CUDA. Document the hardware requirements including minimum specifications for NVIDIA RTX 3060 or better cards. Explain the CUDA setup process including installing CUDA Toolkit 12.x, enabling WHISPER_CUBLAS and GGML_CUDA flags in CMakeLists.txt, and rebuilding with the -CUDA flag. Detail the performance improvements achieved with GPU acceleration including 5-10x speedup on typical models and expected transcription times (3-5s for 30s audio with base.en model). Document the technical implementation including cuBLAS integration, memory management between CPU and GPU, and automatic fallback to CPU when GPU acceleration is unavailable. Include troubleshooting guidance for common CUDA installation issues, driver compatibility problems, and performance verification techniques. Address cost-benefit analysis comparing GPU acceleration investment against performance gains.

wiki_catalogs.name: Visual Feedback System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Direct2D-based visual feedback system covering the floating overlay implementation. Document the GPU-accelerated waveform visualization with real-time audio level display using pill-shaped bars. Explain the state indicator animations including the spinner arc for transcription processing and color-coded status flashes (green for success, red for errors). Detail the overlay positioning system with X/Y coordinate configuration and automatic placement (-1 indicates center). Cover the 60fps rendering loop using WM_TIMER messages and Direct2D target recreation handling for display changes. Document the overlay lifecycle including creation, updates, and automatic hiding. Include customization options for overlay appearance and positioning. Address performance considerations for maintaining smooth 60fps rendering during transcription processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transcriber API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Transcriber class interface. Document the Whisper.cpp integration methods including model loading functions, GPU acceleration controls, and asynchronous transcription capabilities. Detail the transcription workflow methods and callback mechanisms for receiving transcription results. Explain the audio processing integration with the audio manager and data flow patterns. Document configuration options for model selection, language settings, and performance tuning parameters. Cover error handling, fallback mechanisms, and status reporting methods. Include method signatures, parameter descriptions, return values, and usage examples. Address threading considerations, memory management, and performance optimization strategies for real-time transcription.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Flow-On system architecture. Describe the high-level design showing the integration of audio processing, AI transcription, visual feedback, and text injection components. Document the finite state machine pattern used for managing the recording, transcription, and injection phases. Explain the thread model with separate threads for audio capture, AI processing, and UI rendering. Detail the component interactions including the main message loop, audio callback thread, worker thread, and text injection thread. Document the data flow from audio input through PCM capture, buffer management, asynchronous Whisper processing, text formatting, and final text injection. Explain the architectural patterns including Observer pattern for event-driven communication, Factory pattern for component initialization, and Command pattern for thread-safe operations. Include system boundaries showing how external dependencies like whisper.cpp, miniaudio, and Direct2D integrate with the core application. Address cross-cutting concerns like performance optimization, memory management, and error handling strategies. Document the technology stack decisions and their impact on system behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack documentation for Flow-On focused on the technical foundation and architectural choices. Document the primary technologies including C++20 as the core language with modern features like std::atomic FSM and std::thread. Explain the audio processing stack built around miniaudio.h for single-header PCM capture and RMS computation. Detail the Whisper.cpp integration as the offline speech recognition backend with AVX2 optimization and optional CUDA support. Document the text processing pipeline using wregex for UTF-16 aware transformations and cwctype functions for character manipulation. Explain the UI architecture combining Direct2D for GPU-accelerated overlay rendering, Win32 for system tray and windows, and conditional WinUI 3 support. Cover the concurrency model using moodycamel::ReaderWriterQueue for lock-free audio processing and Windows message-based IPC. Document the build system using CMake with AVX2 compilation flags, fast-math optimizations, and static linking. Include dependency management through Git submodules and the external directory structure. Explain platform-specific considerations for Windows API integration, threading models, and performance optimizations. Provide rationale for technology choices and their impact on performance, memory usage, and system requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the component interaction patterns in Flow-On. Document the Observer pattern implementation through Windows message handling and event-driven communication between subsystems. Explain the factory pattern used for component initialization and dependency injection. Detail the command pattern for thread-safe message passing and operation queuing. Document the main message loop coordination showing how WindowProc handles different message types and delegates to appropriate subsystems. Explain the data flow from audio capture through PCM buffer management to asynchronous transcription processing. Cover the integration points between components including the audio manager's callback mechanism, transcriber's completion notifications, and overlay's state synchronization. Document the configuration management flow from dashboard UI changes to persistent settings storage. Include the error propagation mechanisms and how failures in one component affect the overall system state.

wiki_catalogs.name: Overlay System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Overlay System component. Thoroughly explain the Direct2D-based floating overlay implementation, including window creation, transparency handling, and screen positioning logic. Document the animation framework for state transitions (recording, processing, done, error), including timing functions and visual feedback mechanisms. Detail the waveform visualization system, audio level display, and dynamic UI updates. Explain the integration with the main application state machine, message handling for overlay events, and z-order management. Include practical examples of overlay initialization, state management, and custom styling options. Document performance considerations for continuous rendering, GPU acceleration usage, and battery optimization. Address multi-monitor support, DPI scaling, and accessibility features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for memory management optimization in Flow-On's resource-constrained environment. Document the current memory footprint including typical usage (~400MB) and memory allocation patterns for audio buffers, model loading, and transcription processing. Explain the memory-efficient strategies including quantized model loading (ggml-tiny.en.bin at 39MB), circular buffer implementation for audio processing, and optimized data structures for zero-copy operations. Detail the memory management techniques including pre-allocation strategies, garbage collection avoidance, and memory pooling for audio samples. Document the trade-offs between memory usage and performance, including how reduced audio context (512 frames) impacts memory consumption. Include monitoring techniques for memory usage tracking, memory leak prevention strategies, and recommendations for systems with limited RAM. Address memory optimization for different deployment scenarios and hardware configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Text Formatting and Modes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the intelligent text formatting system covering the three operation modes and transformation algorithms. Document the AUTO mode's application detection system for VS Code, Cursor, vim, and terminal environments with automatic camelCase/snake_case transformations. Explain the NORMAL mode's text cleaning including filler word removal (um, uh, ah) and punctuation correction. Detail the CODING mode's advanced transformations including user name → userName, api response → api_response, HTTP method → HTTP_METHOD with technical vocabulary anchoring. Document the four-pass regex cleanup process and UTF-16 awareness for international character support. Cover the mode selection criteria and automatic switching behavior. Include practical examples of before/after transformations and configuration options for customizing formatting behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Overlay API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Overlay class interface. Document the Direct2D-based floating overlay implementation including initialization, rendering methods, and animation controls. Document the window positioning, transparency controls, and display coordination with the main application. Cover the integration with the audio manager for RMS-based visual effects and the formatter for text display. Include method signatures, parameter descriptions, return values, and usage examples. Address graphics performance considerations, DPI scaling support, and cross-monitor compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the core components section focused on the major functional modules that make up Flow-On. wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture

wiki_catalogs.name: Text Formatter

wiki_catalogs.name: Profiling and Benchmarking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for performance profiling and benchmarking methodologies in Flow-On. Explain the profiling tools and techniques available including Windows Performance Analyzer, CPU usage monitoring, and memory usage tracking. Detail the performance metrics to monitor including transcription time per audio length, CPU utilization patterns, GPU utilization (when available), and system resource contention. Document the troubleshooting methodology for identifying performance bottlenecks including systematic elimination of potential causes, background process interference, and hardware limitations. Include best practices for performance regression testing, A/B testing different optimization configurations, and establishing baseline performance expectations. Provide guidance for interpreting benchmark results and making informed decisions about performance trade-offs.

Include practical examples of configuration loading, settings modification, and backup/restore procedures. Address configuration file location, permissions, and cross-user compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Injector API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Injector class interface. Document the text injection functionality including application detection, input simulation, and fallback mechanisms. Detail the Windows API integration for SendInput operations and keyboard event generation. Explain the application context awareness and input method selection. Document the error handling for injection failures and alternative input strategies. Cover the integration with the formatter output and text processing pipeline. Include method signatures, parameter descriptions, return values, and usage examples. Address security considerations, permission requirements, and compatibility with different application types.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Flow-On public interfaces and component APIs. Document the audio manager API including PCM capture functions, buffer management methods, and audio device configuration. Detail the transcriber API covering Whisper integration methods, model loading functions, GPU acceleration controls, and transcription result callbacks. Document the overlay API including Direct2D rendering methods, animation controls, state management functions, and visual feedback mechanisms. Explain the dashboard API for settings management, history tracking, and UI component interactions. Cover the formatter API including text processing methods, formatting mode controls, and context-aware transformation functions. Document the snippet engine API for text substitution, trigger management, and expansion logic. Detail the configuration manager API for settings persistence, validation, and runtime updates. Explain the injector API for text injection operations, application detection, and fallback mechanisms. Include method signatures, parameter descriptions, return values, and usage examples. Document event handling patterns and callback mechanisms. Address thread safety considerations and synchronization requirements. Provide integration examples for extending functionality using the public APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the troubleshooting section focused on diagnosing and resolving common Flow-On issues. Document systematic approaches to troubleshooting hotkey problems including VK code verification, conflict resolution, and registration status checks. Address audio-related issues covering device detection, driver conflicts, service restart procedures, and input device configuration. Explain Whisper model troubleshooting including download failures, disk space requirements, cache management, and model corruption recovery. Document installer and deployment issues including NSIS requirements, PATH configuration, and installation permissions. Cover performance-related problems including GPU acceleration setup, memory usage optimization, and CPU bottleneck identification. Address system integration issues including registry problems, autostart configuration, and Windows compatibility. Include diagnostic commands and tools for gathering system information. Provide step-by-step resolution procedures for common error scenarios. Document logging and debugging techniques for advanced troubleshooting. Include recovery procedures for corrupted installations and configuration files. Address platform-specific issues and compatibility considerations.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Flow-On: Real-Time Speech-to-Text Assistant for Windows

## Project Introduction

Flow-On is a high-performance Windows desktop application that transforms speech into text in real-time using advanced AI models. The project delivers a seamless dictation experience with minimal latency, featuring a sophisticated audio pipeline, GPU-accelerated transcription, and intelligent text injection capabilities.

### Purpose Statement
Flow-On addresses the need for fast, accurate speech-to-text conversion on Windows desktops, providing developers and writers with instant voice-to-keyboard functionality through a lightweight system tray application.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Performance**: Achieve sub-3 second transcription latency for typical dictation scenarios
- **Cross-platform Compatibility**: Support both CPU and NVIDIA GPU acceleration transparently
- **Minimal Intrusion**: Provide unobtrusive overlay feedback with Direct2D rendering
- **Developer-Focused**: Integrate seamlessly with code editors and writing environments

### Target Audience
- Professional writers and content creators
- Software developers requiring rapid code documentation
- Power users seeking efficient voice-to-text workflows
- Windows desktop users prioritizing performance and reliability

## Technical Architecture

Flow-On employs a modular, event-driven architecture built around a central finite state machine that coordinates multiple specialized subsystems.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Main Entry Point] --> B[Audio Manager]
    A --> C[Transcriber]
    A --> D[Overlay System]
    A --> E[Dashboard]
    A --> F[Configuration Manager]
    
    B --> G[Miniaudio Capture]
    C --> H[Whisper.cpp Engine]
    D --> I[Direct2D Rendering]
    E --> J[WinUI 3/Win32 UI]
    F --> K[JSON Settings]
    
    L[System Tray] --> A
    M[Hotkey Handler] --> A
    N[Message Loop] --> A
    
    A --> O[Text Injector]
    O --> P[SendInput API]
    O --> Q[Clipboard Fallback]
```

### Component Breakdown

The application follows a layered architecture with clear separation of concerns:

**Core Layer**: Event-driven state machine managing application lifecycle
**Audio Layer**: Real-time PCM capture with noise reduction and VAD simulation  
**Processing Layer**: Asynchronous transcription with GPU/CPU fallback
**Presentation Layer**: Visual feedback through floating overlay and dashboard
**Integration Layer**: System tray integration and text injection

### Design Patterns

- **Finite State Machine**: Atomic state transitions coordinate recording, transcription, and injection phases
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven communication between subsystems
- **Factory Pattern**: Modular component initialization with dependency injection
- **Command Pattern**: Message-based IPC for thread-safe operations

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application bootstraps through a sophisticated initialization sequence:

**Primary Entry**: `src/main.cpp` - Contains the complete application lifecycle including hotkey registration, tray integration, and component orchestration

**Windows Integration**: `flow-on.cpp` - Standard Win32 application entry point with message handling and window management

### Core Modules

**Audio Processing**: `src/audio_manager.cpp` - Real-time PCM capture using miniaudio with 16kHz mono sampling and automatic noise detection

**Speech Recognition**: `src/transcriber.cpp` - Asynchronous Whisper integration with GPU acceleration and single-flight request gating

**Visual Feedback**: `src/overlay.cpp` - Direct2D-based floating overlay with animated waveform visualization and state indicators

**User Interface**: `src/dashboard.cpp` - WinUI 3/Win32 hybrid dashboard for settings management and transcription history

**Configuration Management**: `src/config_manager.cpp` - JSON-based settings persistence with autostart functionality

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration**: `CMakeLists.txt` - Comprehensive CMake setup with AVX2 optimizations, OpenMP threading, and CUDA support

**Runtime Settings**: `assets/settings.default.json` - JSON schema defining hotkey preferences, model selection, and text snippets

**System Integration**: Windows Registry integration for autostart functionality and system tray persistence

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**:
- whisper.cpp: AI speech recognition engine with quantized models
- miniaudio: Cross-platform audio capture and playback
- Direct2D/DirectWrite: Hardware-accelerated 2D graphics rendering
- ReaderWriterQueue: Lock-free concurrent queue implementation

**Build Dependencies**:
- CMake 3.20+: Modern CMake with C++20 support
- Visual Studio 2022: Primary development environment
- NSIS: Windows installer generation

### Integration Points

**System APIs**:
- Windows Audio Session API for microphone access
- SendInput API for text injection
- Shell_NotifyIcon for tray integration
- Windows Registry for persistent settings

**Third-party Integration**:
- HuggingFace for model downloads
- NVIDIA CUDA for GPU acceleration (optional)
- Windows App SDK for WinUI 3 dashboard

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class AppState {
        <<enumeration>>
        IDLE
        RECORDING
        TRANSCRIBING
        INJECTING
    }
    
    class AudioManager {
        +init(callback)
        +startCapture()
        +drainBuffer()
        +getRMS()
    }
    
    class Transcriber {
        +init(modelPath)
        +transcribeAsync(pcm, hwnd)
        +isBusy()
    }
    
    class Overlay {
        +init(hInst)
        +setState(state)
        +pushRMS(rms)
    }
    
    class Dashboard {
        +init(hInst, ownerHwnd)
        +show()
        +addEntry(entry)
    }
    
    class ConfigManager {
        +load()
        +save()
        +applyAutostart(path)
    }
    
    AppState --> AudioManager
    AppState --> Transcriber
    AppState --> Overlay
    Dashboard --> ConfigManager
    Transcriber --> WhisperModel
    Overlay --> Direct2D
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Dictation**: Press Alt+V to start recording, release to trigger immediate transcription with visual feedback

**Intelligent Text Processing**: Automatic formatting based on detected application context (code vs prose)

**GPU Acceleration**: Transparent fallback between NVIDIA CUDA and CPU processing for optimal performance

**Visual Feedback System**: Animated overlay showing recording status, transcription progress, and completion states

**Smart Text Injection**: Seamless insertion of transcribed text into active applications with fallback to clipboard

### Implementation Highlights

**Performance Optimization**: `src/transcriber.cpp` - Customizable Whisper parameters for balancing speed and accuracy

**Memory Management**: Sophisticated buffer management with secure zeroing to prevent sensitive data retention

**Error Handling**: Comprehensive error recovery with user-friendly notifications and graceful degradation

**Cross-platform Considerations**: Win32 API abstraction enabling future platform expansion

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle: Initial State
    
    Idle --> Recording: Alt+V Press
    Recording --> Transcribing: Release Hotkey
    Recording --> Idle: Hotkey Conflict
    
    Transcribing --> Processing: Audio Ready
    Processing --> Injecting: Transcription Complete
    Processing --> Error: Audio Issues
    
    Injecting --> Idle: Text Inserted
    Error --> Idle: User Resolved
    
    Idle --> Dashboard: Tray Double-click
    Dashboard --> Idle: Settings Saved
```

**Latency Optimization**: The system achieves sub-3 second end-to-end latency through optimized audio buffering, asynchronous processing, and efficient GPU utilization

**Quality Control**: Intelligent audio gating prevents transcription of short, noisy recordings while maintaining responsiveness

**Extensibility**: Plugin-like architecture supporting custom text processing rules and application-specific formatting

---

**Sources:**
- [flow-on.cpp](flow-on.cpp)
- [flow-on.h](flow-on.h) 
- [CMakeLists.txt](CMakeLists.txt)
- [src/main.cpp](src/main.cpp)
- [src/audio_manager.h](src/audio_manager.h)
- [src/transcriber.h](src/transcriber.h)
- [src/overlay.h](src/overlay.h)
- [src/dashboard.h](src/dashboard.h)
- [src/config_manager.h](src/config_manager.h)
- [assets/settings.default.json](assets/settings.default.json)
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [PERFORMANCE.md](PERFORMANCE.md)
- [build.ps1](build.ps1)
- [installer/flow-on.nsi](installer/flow-on.nsi)

</blog>
